It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with potential health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, as well as antioxidant and cardio-protective effects. The bioactive constituents of Ruehssia macroglossa include alkaloids, such as annonacin and annonaine, as well as other compounds like flavonoids and terpenoids.
